steps.title: Spot the figurative language
steps.content: The key signal is "welcoming arms" — the trees are personified as actively extending arms to greet Shakti. This human-like gesture of welcome is the central figurative move the question asks about.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The personification frames the landscape as greeting Shakti warmly, implying she belongs there. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — It suggests that Shakti feels uncomfortable near the river.: The arms are "welcoming," not threatening or unsettling; nothing in the personification signals discomfort. ✗
  • Choice B — It indicates that Shakti has lost her sense of direction in the woods.: A landscape that extends welcoming arms is oriented toward her, not disorienting her; no confusion is implied. ✗
  • Choice C — It emphasizes Shakti's sense of belonging in the landscape.: When a place "welcomes" someone with open arms, it signals that person genuinely belongs there — exactly what this personification conveys. ✓
  • Choice D — It conveys Shakti's appreciation for her long-term friendships.: The personification focuses on her relationship with the landscape, not with people; friendships are not referenced. ✗
That is the correct answer.
